description: Bid 2D/2H over 1NT to show a 5+ card major and let opener declare
purpose: Right-side the contract so the strong 1NT hand stays hidden, while guaranteeing a trump fit when responder has a 5+ card major

authority.label: ACBL SAYC — Jacoby Transfers are a core SAYC convention, standardized

teaching.tradeoff: Using 2D and 2H as transfers means you can't play in a natural 2D or 2H contract.
teaching.principle: Transfer principle: let the strong hand be declarer to protect tenaces from the opening lead.

commonMistakes.text: Do not transfer with only a four-card major.
commonMistakes.reason: Transfers promise a five-card suit, so a shorter major belongs in Stayman or in natural notrump choices.

commonMistakes.text: Do not stop after opener completes the transfer when your hand is stronger than a signoff.
commonMistakes.reason: Responder's rebid is what shows invite, game, or slam interest after the transfer is accepted.

commonMistakes.text: Do not rebid the major with exactly five balanced invitational values.
commonMistakes.reason: The balanced invite should go through 2NT so opener can choose between notrump and the major.

explanationEntries.displayText: Eligible for Jacoby Transfer
Transfer is the preferred convention
Transfer target suit determined by longest major
Opener has 3+ hearts to fit responder's suit
explanationEntries.contrastiveDisplayText: Opener lacks heart fit (fewer than 3 hearts)

explanationEntries.displayText: Opener has 3+ spades to fit responder's suit
explanationEntries.contrastiveDisplayText: Opener lacks spade fit (fewer than 3 spades)

explanationEntries.displayText: Transfer to hearts: bid 2♦ to show 5+ hearts
Transfer to spades: bid 2♥ to show 5+ spades
Accept transfer: bid the target major (hearts)
Accept transfer: bid the target major (spades)
Jump to 3♥: maximum notrump with four-card heart support
Jump to 3♠: maximum notrump with four-card spade support
Sign off in hearts: weak hand, pass and play in the major
Raise to 4♥: game values with 5+ hearts
Bid 3NT: game values, offers opener choice between 3NT and 4♥
Raise to 3♥: invitational with 6+ hearts, opener decides game
Bid 2NT: invitational with exactly 5 hearts, opener decides
Bid 2♠: invitational with 5 hearts and 5 spades, non-forcing
Bid 3♣: game forcing with 5+ hearts and 4+ clubs
Bid 3♦: game forcing with 5+ hearts and 4+ diamonds
Bid 3♠: singleton/void in spades with slam interest
Bid 4NT: quantitative slam invite in hearts or notrump
Sign off in spades: weak hand, pass and play in the major
Raise to 4♠: game values with 5+ spades
Bid 3NT: game values, offers opener choice between 3NT and 4♠
Raise to 3♠: invitational with 6+ spades, opener decides game
Bid 2NT: invitational with exactly 5 spades, opener decides
Bid 3♣: game forcing with 5+ spades and 4+ clubs
Bid 3♦: game forcing with 5+ spades and 4+ diamonds
Bid 3♥: 5-5 majors with slam interest (stronger than 4♥)
Bid 4♥: game values with 5+ spades and 5+ hearts
Bid 4NT: quantitative slam invite in spades or notrump
Bid 3♥: choose hearts with a minimum after the 5-5 invitational relay
Bid 4♥: accept the 5-5 invitational relay by choosing hearts
Bid 3♠: choose spades with a minimum and a spade fit
Bid 4♠: accept the 5-5 invitational relay by choosing spades
Bid 3NT: no heart fit after responder's game-forcing side suit
Bid 4♥: take the known heart fit after responder's game-forcing side suit
Bid 3NT: no spade fit after responder's game-forcing side suit
Bid 4♠: take the known spade fit after responder's game-forcing side suit
Bid 4♥: sign off opposite the splinter without enough extra strength for slam
Bid 6♥: accept the splinter and place the contract in small slam
Bid 4♠: sign off opposite the 5-5 slam try
Bid 6♠: accept the 5-5 slam try and place the contract in small slam
Pass 4NT: decline the quantitative invitation with a non-maximum notrump
Bid 6NT: accept the quantitative invitation with a maximum notrump
Pass 4NT: decline the quantitative invitation after the spade transfer
Bid 6NT: accept the quantitative invitation after the spade transfer
Correct to 4♥: opener has 3+ hearts, prefer the major game
Pass 3NT: opener lacks heart fit, stay in notrump
Correct to 4♠: opener has 3+ spades, prefer the major game
Pass 3NT: opener lacks spade fit, stay in notrump
Accept heart invite: bid 4♥ with maximum and heart fit
Accept heart invite: bid 3NT with maximum but no heart fit
Sign off in 3♥: minimum hand but 3+ hearts (fit found)
Decline heart invite: pass 2NT with minimum and no heart fit
Accept spade invite: bid 4♠ with maximum and spade fit
Accept spade invite: bid 3NT with maximum but no spade fit
Sign off in 3♠: minimum hand but 3+ spades (fit found)
Decline spade invite: pass 2NT with minimum and no spade fit
Accept heart invite raise: bid 4♥ with maximum, fit and extras
Decline heart invite raise: pass 3♥ with minimum
Accept spade invite raise: bid 4♠ with maximum, fit and extras
Decline spade invite raise: pass 3♠ with minimum

teachingLabel.name: Transfer to hearts
teachingLabel.summary: Bid 2D to ask opener to bid 2H, keeping the strong hand as declarer

teachingLabel.name: Transfer to spades
teachingLabel.summary: Bid 2H to ask opener to bid 2S, keeping the strong hand as declarer

teachingLabel.name: Accept transfer to hearts
teachingLabel.summary: Complete the transfer by bidding 2H as requested

clauses.rationale: maximum of a 15-17 notrump opening — the only hands that can afford a level jump over a possible weak hand opposite
super-accept requires four-card support so the ninth trump is guaranteed

teachingLabel.name: 3H super-accept
teachingLabel.summary: Jump to 3H with a maximum notrump and four-card heart support

teachingLabel.name: Accept transfer to spades
teachingLabel.summary: Complete the transfer by bidding 2S as requested

clauses.rationale: maximum of a 15-17 notrump opening — the only hands that can afford a level jump over a possible weak hand opposite
super-accept requires four-card support so the ninth trump is guaranteed

teachingLabel.name: 3S super-accept
teachingLabel.summary: Jump to 3S with a maximum notrump and four-card spade support

summaryCard.trigger: Partner opens one notrump and you hold a five-card major.
summaryCard.partnership: Two diamonds transfers partner to hearts and two hearts transfers partner to spades as peer major-suit transfers; the conventional act is picking the rank below the intended major so the strong hand declares.

whenToUse.gloss: Use transfers with a weak five-card major to stop low.
Use transfers before inviting or bidding game with a five-card major.
Use transfers before any slam move with a five-card major.

workedAuctions.label: Weak heart signoff keeps the auction low

calls.rationale: Opener shows a balanced notrump opening.
Responder transfers to hearts with a weak hand and a five-card suit.
Opener completes the transfer as requested.
Responder signs off in the major at the cheapest level.

workedAuctions.label: Balanced spade invite lets opener choose the strain

calls.rationale: Opener starts with the notrump opening.
Responder shows an invitational balanced hand with exactly five spades.

workedAuctions.label: Heart transfer can launch a splinter-style slam try

calls.rationale: Opener declares the balanced notrump range.
Responder transfers to hearts before describing slam interest.
Responder shows spade shortness with slam values in a heart fit.

workedAuctions.label: Counter-example: a flat transfer hand should not pass a super-accept into hearts

calls.rationale: Opener shows a balanced notrump opening.
Responder starts with the heart transfer on a flat 4-3-3-3 hand.
Opener super-accepts with four-card heart support and a maximum 1NT (17 HCP).
Passing is the mistake. A flat 4-3-3-3 hand has no ruffing value, so 3NT is categorically better than playing 3H.

items.ourAction: Check the partnership agreement; the common default is systems-on after interference, while many pairs switch to runouts or redouble-based escapes.
items.note: If systems are on, transfers keep their usual meaning; if they are off, responder uses the partnership's agreed escape ladder instead.

items.opponentAction: Overcall of two of a minor
items.ourAction: The transfer structure is disrupted; use the partnership's cue-bid or lebensohl style agreements instead.
items.note: Natural major bids may now be more practical than trying to force the old transfer map.

items.opponentAction: Overcall at two of a major or higher
items.ourAction: Transfers disappear and responder competes naturally or with separate competitive methods.
items.note: The interference takes away the room that the transfer continuations need.

items.recommendation: Start with the transfer to hearts.
items.note: Weak hands pass two hearts. Invitational hands use two notrump with a balanced five-card suit or three hearts with extra length. Stronger hands choose three notrump or four hearts, a side-suit game force, three spades as a splinter, or four notrump quantitative.

items.recommendation: Start with the transfer to spades.
items.note: Weak hands pass two spades. Invitational hands use two notrump with a balanced five-card suit or three spades with extra length. Stronger hands choose three notrump or four spades, a side-suit game force, four hearts with game-only five-five majors, three hearts as a slam try, or four notrump quantitative.

relatedLinks.discriminator: Use Stayman with a four-card major; use transfers when responder already knows the major is five cards or longer.
Jacoby Four-Way keeps the transfer core but adds minor-suit and stronger two-notrump continuations.
Use Smolen after Stayman when responder is five-four in the majors and wants opener to declare the game fit.

definitions.description: Transfer target suit (hearts, spades, or none)
Eligible for Jacoby transfer (5+ card major)
Opener has 3+ hearts (fit with responder's 5-card suit)
Opener has 3+ spades (fit with responder's 5-card suit)
